title: spergularia rubra subsp. salina
intro: Spergularia rubra subsp. salina, also known as the red sand-spurrey, is a perennial plant species of the family Caryophyllaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and terpenes. The plant has been reported to have several health benefits, including the potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as exhibit antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities. Herbal preparations of Spergularia rubra subsp. salina are typically made through infusions or decoctions of the plant's leaves and stems, and are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
